name: Charlie Parker
abstract: Charles Parker Jr. (August 29, 1920 – March 12, 1955), nicknamed "Bird" or "Yardbird", was an American jazz saxophonist, bandleader, and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Mondello
about_quotes.evidence: teachers say, you know, “I asked this kid about Charlie Parker and you know he didn’t know anything about Charlie —” well the kid’s a sixth grader or seventh grader, whatever grader he happens to be. You know it’s our job to at least introduce not only who and what, when and where, but why — why Cha
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Mondello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: I remember being asked to play with Charlie Parker, be in the band backing him up at the Broadway Theatre... I got my nerve up and went stood aside the stage and said, 'Could I play something?' He said, 'Sure, come on up.' And I found myself sitting on the stage with Bird
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howie Shear
about_quotes.evidence: But yet it’s not a dumbed-down version, but’s what players need to know to improvise quite well actually. So it’s not simplified for just like the novice or the grade school kid. It’s really like Charlie Parker played jazz and after him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howie Shear interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: I think that in our previous interview I said that my ultimate dream when I was in Cuba after I became a musician and I knew that jazz was the music that I wanted to play, my ultimate dreams was watching my heroes one day on video.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ignacio Berroa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 22, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eric Alexander
about_quotes.evidence: "You know we’re not all Mozart or Charlie Parker." / "if I give one kid “Charlie Parker with Strings,” and say this is the only thing you’re allowed to listen for six months. I want you to learn every note that he plays"
about_quotes.doc_title: Eric Alexander interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: I know that Lou plays a lot of Charlie Parker. ... I started to sing the Charlie Parker solo, with lyrics by Eddie Jefferson. ... 'Obviously you are familiar with that Charlie Parker I mean you nailed it in my key.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]
